name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Three years ago.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
I was ready to fight to protect our democracy on January 6th
#

Peter -
Three years ago, emboldened by President Trump and people like Kari Lake, our democracy was pushed to the limit by the treasonous actions of violent insurrectionists.
I was there.
[](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan24.RCwesN&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=1&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)
As a Marine Corps veteran, I never imagined having to use my training to protect myself and my colleagues on the House floor.
I never imagined teaching my House colleagues how to put on a gas mask and regulate their breathing.
I never imagined I’d stay behind after the Capitol security came in to do one more sweep for colleagues who might have frozen and gone to cocoon somewhere hard to find.
I never imagined I would ever have to teach my staff how to knock on the door of our office a certain way to signal it was them and not some attacker — something we did in the Marine Corps when surrounded by insurgents and you didn’t know who was good and who was bad.
And I never imagined I’d have to text members of the House and urge them not to get on buses ready to evacuate us from the Capitol because if we left we’d lose the building to the coup.
The truth is, I was ready to fight to protect our democracy on January 6th. I’m proud that Democrats returned to the floor and certified the election, and I’m proud of the work we’ve done to strengthen our elections and our democracy since then.
But the threat has not gone away. It might even be greater now with election deniers like Kari Lake running for Senate, spreading the same lies and conspiracies that caused January 6th in the first place.
Nothing less than our democracy itself is at stake this year. This anniversary is a gut check moment — are we going to step up and fight for it, or are we going to let it slip away?
Marines are the first to the fight, and I’m not backing down. You can count on me to be on the frontlines, fighting as hard as I can to keep election deniers out of the Senate. But I can’t do it without you.
[**So please, can you contribute $3 today to my campaign for U.S. Senate in Arizona? Everything is going to come down to Arizona this year, and with Kari Lake running against me, we can’t let up. Not one bit.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan24.RCwesN&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=2&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1046?c0=ruben-em-jan24.Z_9ALj&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=22&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)**

| | **[Donate $50](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1039?c0=ruben-em-jan24.J-vKsf&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=23&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1040?c0=ruben-em-jan24.DfsFQf&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=24&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1041?c0=ruben-em-jan24.7U1Rob&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=25&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1044?c0=ruben-em-jan24.7CeOzz&refcode=em240106-1-30&t=26&akid=1664%2E734936%2EqWEZ5R)**
---

These are difficult times. But this country is our home. And there is nowhere I would rather be than here, fighting for our future. With you.
Semper Fi,
Ruben Gallego

body:
I’m dedicated to safeguarding our democracy and ensuring that an attack like January 6 never happens again, but I need your help.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76595415/447899137/1790176412?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzgyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMDliOTMwMC1hM2FjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5DaKZ7fZPCydO1N79ghAEEgQn7J0Gv4RREeUe4qrnmk=&emci=5e93b0cf-84aa-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=c09b9300-a3ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=23311593) | Peter,

Today – on the third anniversary of the January 6, 2021 attack on the United States Capitol – we are reminded of the fragility of our democracy and the importance of standing up against those who seek to undermine it.

Last year, I faced an opponent who denied the results of the 2020 election, a stance that _directly_ threatens the foundation of our democratic process. There are candidates across the country on the ballot this November who continue to attack our democracy and refuse to acknowledge the results of a free and fair election.

**The continued prevalence of this rhetoric is a haunting reminder that we must remain vigilant, united, and committed to protecting the values that make our country strong.**

I’m dedicated to safeguarding our democracy and ensuring that an attack like January 6 never happens again, but I need your help.

Will you join me in this crucial fight to defend our democracy? name: Donald J. Trump
subject: Crooked Joe thinks he’s George Washington?!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Save America Joint Fundraising Committee, a joint fundraising committee composed of and authorized by Donald J
body:
The same Crooked Joe who’s tearing our Free Republic to shreds.
| | | [ ](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kc2umzAQhZ_G7EBmbGNYsEiKsonaRavbn1U0jE0yV9ggMEX37Sui3G6_czQ6nwbDPPLw0Qp7FgAPdqsAEOokAK76_Ptbdf117fLvqMu8q4zejxS-CID0Mfv_1deVwgfk8aC2y1wLVANR5tvSSm0MNGWdPdrSNQNKJATXa1TO1WboTV97aiygKjNuQYKWpaxKbUxpC_Cke6Sqb-RAUtdCy51j4aaIo3tPyxbmgqaQje0jpXl9TroIuKyetsUXO8fFu6NxMPzrcwx-YcL8feKY8mGLbkFeOd5zmkLglLwXcBl8g0Sqr420ZAwKddlSuAXveAtCdS_Z6oDrtC3kheqIb_hihGFGvkehuk-hz2SKycckVFdKW1sB1S2O7ITq5v7N_tx_vMH9CZ__UN3pPM58-lN9zZbWzTuPI6-VFVrejwmH2b8AAAD__yPOjXo)
---
Friend,
Yesterday, in one of the most ridiculous speeches I’ve ever heard, Crooked Joe Biden likened himself to George Washington...
...And likened his reelection campaign to the war for freedom that Washington led to secure our independence.
_This is the very same Joe Biden, who is actively attempting to**ERASE** your sacred right to vote by having my name **REMOVED** from the 2024 ballot... _
_The same man who had his leading political opponent (yours truly) unlawfully**ARRESTED** FOUR TIMES, and is trying to jail me for life as an innocent man... _
_And the same Crook who had his Department of “Justice” gag and censor me!_
Does that sound like our nation’s hero – George Washington – to you?
In his speech, Biden even declared that _“America made a vow — never again would we bow down to a king.”_
And yet, on this very day, I look at the Crook currently occupying the White House and see no difference between Joe Biden and a Marxist Third World Dictator desperate to remain in power.
**Crooked Joe is the TRUE threat to democracy.**
He is the man actively trying to destroy our once great country from within... _A man who is more than happy to NULLIFY the votes of a majority of Americans before they even have the chance to cast a ballot! _
But worst of all, Crooked Joe didn’t _just_ falsely praise himself in his absurd speech...
**He attacked _OUR_ movement – he attacked YOU. **

clean_body: The same Crooked Joe who’s tearing our Free Republic to shreds. | | | Friend, Yesterday, in one of the most ridiculous speeches I’ve ever heard, Crooked Joe Biden likened himself to George Washington... ...And likened his reelection campaign to the war for freedom that Washington led to secure our independence. This is the very same Joe Biden, who is actively attempting toERASE your sacred right to vote by having my name REMOVED from the 2024 ballot... The same man who had his leading political opponent (yours truly) unlawfullyARRESTED FOUR TIMES, and is trying to jail me for life as an innocent man... And the same Crook who had his Department of “Justice” gag and censor me! Does that sound like our nation’s hero – George Washington – to you? In his speech, Biden even declared that “America made a vow — never again would we bow down to a king.” And yet, on this very day, I look at the Crook currently occupying the White House and see no difference between Joe Biden and a Marxist Third World Dictator desperate to remain in power. Crooked Joe is the TRUE threat to democracy. He is the man actively trying to destroy our once great country from within... A man who is more than happy to NULLIFY the votes of a majority of Americans before they even have the chance to cast a ballot! But worst of all, Crooked Joe didn’t just falsely praise himself in his absurd speech... He attacked OUR movement – he attacked YOU. And right now, you can PROVE to Crooked Joe and the Radical Democrats that the very same Americans he attacks are the REAL saviors of our country. Because patriots like YOU willNEVER , EVER SURRENDER to his Third World tyranny! | | Thank you, God bless you, and God bless America! | Donald J. name: Matthew Diemer
subject: Allow me to reintroduce myself
body:
| |
---
Peter, My name is Matthew Diemer, and I’m officially running for Congress in Ohio’s 7th Congressional District. I grew up right here in Ohio watching my parents struggle to make ends meet. I’ve always wanted to serve this community the best way I can and help everyone in the community thrive. Today, that means running for and winning a seat in Congress so that I can represent us accurately in D.C. Three years ago today, far-right extremists stormed the United States Capitol building in an attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election. **Representative Max Miller helped plan that attack.****** January 6th, 2021 was one of the darkest days in American history, and Max Miller played a huge role in making that day happen. I’m running for Congress because a man who helped plan a violent insurrection to overturn the results of a democratic election isn’t fit to represent us in the Capitol. We deserve better. Ohio’s 7th deserves a Representative who truly cares about the peoples’ needs and wants instead of trying to turn citizens against each other. Max Miller already has a huge fundraising advantage, and we need to raise a strong total out of the gate to build up an operation that can match Max’s. [Will you donate $10, $25, $50, $100, or any other amount to help us get our campaign started on a strong note?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wDZWX9f_QGXYt8-x-WTsfwau4AGEzXof0WQnM45wbhzdJMh-oeVFJNdsxZUh-DFoHK85PK5VRH2pP6i4cmOsovX6Xdyknw3m9yG-H_sXAf5Sipqoe0qfHDkHZHfdQz6Hdt44bIo41w8x-UtKSwDZ3XbN71KeD3VV5DHFnCr3NoNNuQKgYvKXg5dSDfXU0ppPykhA6iQvyirdg0ctrqYEUU8tUySe7L1SEf09znlBfcpuj5_TMqoZqiJmWzQQIiu-i_yzbLmAU9d-wCgY8dQOGKWEoB_S0s2XuLvYCecxXcV-CMOlXkXvmZO_qmKtdKxRmCASLsI5BlCTxiB_A168M4/42r/DWHECckxSea_vRVtW6WTpw/h0/yy-lwEQcvsIWni-y7sIqVVKFS6-vZLm09RmUn1-fe34)

name: Janelle Stelson
subject: Three years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Janelle Stelson

Janelle Stelson has spent her career advocating for truth and integrity, and now she's running to unseat extremist Scott Perry and represent Pennsylvania's 10th District in Congress. **January 6, 2021: A date that will forever be remembered as one of the darkest days in American history.**

Three years ago, an angry mob of insurrectionists descended on the Capitol, forcing members of Congress to take cover. **Seven people lost their lives because of that attack.**

Hours later, 147 Republicans voted to overturn the results of the 2020 election. Many Republicans seem to think we should just forget about the violence on that fateful day— but the American people will never forget.

We won’t forget that Congressman Scott Perry voted to throw out our votes and then conspired with key members of Trump’s team to try to prevent President Biden from taking office. **As a former Trump staffer put it, Perry was “central to the planning of Jan. 6.”**

The FBI then seized Perry’s phone and he’s spent the last two years dodging subpoenas for his involvement.

Congressman Perry’s actions following the 2020 election helped inspire me to run. Pennsylvania, and our country, cannot afford another two years of Scott Perry.

He’s already tried to throw out our votes once— and he will try again. **[Will you pitch in $10 or $25 now to help send me to Congress to defend our votes— and fight against sedition and chaos?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597604/447903025/-1786018290?amount=25&source=SBD_JS_EM_FR_010624_1_1_Jan6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3MTMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzIzY2Y0NWYtYTRhYy1lZTExLWJlYTEtMDAyMjQ4MjIzN2RhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aVx0BMNPLHvK_h-HKLHI8MVIrCE7A_gBp12mWtEmyAc=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

name: Andy Kim
subject: The suit I wore
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
Believe it or not, I found it on the sale rack at J. Crew, not long after the 2020 election.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597475/447902830/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzEyZGM0Mi1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ycnUov-G5EEW6MvgTpm5--p4GbhuOvcPKuGYMWS7t9A=&emci=f62b32c9-71aa-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=6312dc42-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=26261389)

| | Believe it or not, I found this suit on the sale rack at J. Crew, not long after the 2020 election. I liked the bold blue color and thought I might wear it to the inauguration a few weeks later. When I donated it to the Smithsonian, the knees were dusted with dirt from the night I spent cleaning trash off of the Capitol's rotunda floor on January 6. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597477/447902835/1416144778?refcode=2024-01-06-jansix&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=6312dc42-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzEyZGM0Mi1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ycnUov-G5EEW6MvgTpm5--p4GbhuOvcPKuGYMWS7t9A=) _Holding my bright blue suit, perhaps for the last time, before I donated it to the Smithsonian._ Honestly, I had seriously considered throwing the suit away until the Smithsonian called. That day still brings such terrible memories for me; I knew I'd never wear it again. **But the story of January 6 isn't just about violence, insurrection, and trauma.** Like our country's story, that day ended full of hope, resilience, civility, and service. Even the simple step I took to help clean up that trash the insurrectionists left behind sparked something much more significant. In the weeks that followed, I received thousands of cards and letters from folks across the country who were inspired or comforted by what they saw. I still cherish every single one. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597478/447902836/1416144778?refcode=2024-01-06-jansix&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=6312dc42-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzEyZGM0Mi1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ycnUov-G5EEW6MvgTpm5--p4GbhuOvcPKuGYMWS7t9A=) _I never imagined that something as simple and reflexive as cleaning up a mess I saw could become inspirational to those like the artist who sent me this wonderful illustration._ Those cards remind me how strong this country is, and how strong we can be when we come together. Right now, our partisan divide can seem so deep that it threatens to tear us apart. We saw it on full display on January 6, and we must ensure nothing like that ever happens again. That means working with Congress to protect our Capitol, but it also means all of us working together to find common ground. **That common ground is closer than you might realize.** I can clearly see the disconnect between the partisan wars being waged in DC, the in-fighting within the GOP's own party, and the priorities of my friends and neighbors in New Jersey. We are honestly all just exhausted by the endless cycles of misinformation and outrage. It's a mess, for sure—and it's all of ours to clean up together. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597482/447902838/1416144778?refcode=2024-01-06-jansix&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=6312dc42-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzEyZGM0Mi1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ycnUov-G5EEW6MvgTpm5--p4GbhuOvcPKuGYMWS7t9A=) I'm raising my two Jersey boys in the same community that welcomed my parents from South Korea. Everything I have done in Congress is to keep the promise I made to help build them a better world than the one we inherited — **and now, I am running for U.S. Senate to bring that same integrity and an emphasis on putting people first.** That's why the movement we're building is focused on civility and service, starting in the same New Jersey community that raised me and stretching out to every corner of our country. [**I'm ready to help bring our country together and fix what's broken. That's why I'm running for Senate to restore integrity and honor to our government and I'm humbly asking you to join me in this cause. Can I count on you to chip in to support my grassroots campaign?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76597483/447902842/1416144778?refcode=2024-01-06-jansix&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=6312dc42-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzEyZGM0Mi1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ycnUov-G5EEW6MvgTpm5--p4GbhuOvcPKuGYMWS7t9A=) More soon, _Andy_ **** | **Express Donate:** **If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:**

body:
Legislative session gets underway with low expectations | | | |
---
| | |
# Capitol Report
January 6, 2024
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| **Contact Senator Roberts** 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329
Jefferson City, MO 65101
(573) 751-4415
Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov
---
| | | | | | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=08f760f8d9&e=a088917f89)
---
| | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=b6a2c13762&e=a088917f89)
---
| | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=4d29173b2c&e=a088917f89)
---
|
---
| **Legislative session gets underway with low expectations**
The 2024 legislative session began Jan. 3 with Republican leaders trying to strike an optimistic tone but hardline conservatives in the Senate making it clear they intend to continue the obstructionism that led to historically low numbers of bills winning final passage in each of the last three years.

Although Republicans hold supermajorities in both chambers, GOP infighting has sharply limited their ability to enact legislation. The Senate hardliners first organized in 2021 as the Conservative Caucus but claimed heading into the 2023 session they were disbanding to instead work from within the larger Senate Republican Caucus. In practice, however, nothing changed and the group continued block legislation supported by more mainstream Republicans.

This year, they’re operating under the Freedom Caucus banner and began the first day of session by holding the Senate floor for more than an hour to air grievances against GOP leaders. The hardliners started to resume their criticisms on the session’s second day but were cut off when the Senate majority leader moved to adjourn the chamber until next week.

For their part, Democratic lawmakers noted that the limited number of bills to win passage last year included several the party had long advocated for and earned bipartisan support. They look to build upon those successes this year to push for targeted tax relief for working families, bolster reproductive rights, improve public school teacher salaries and reduce violent crime by strengthening Missouri’s lax gun laws, among other issues.

With an initiative petition being circulated to put a proposed amendment to the state constitution protecting abortion rights on the November ballot, House Republicans said they will make a last-ditch effort to require supermajority approval to ratify amendments. However, any changes proposed by lawmakers also would need to be approved by voters to take effect, and voters in others states have proven resistant to limiting their own power.

Even if a higher vote threshold made it on the August ballot and won voter approval, the courts likely would decide whether the changes applied to measures on the November ballot since doing so would amount to changing the rules in the middle of the process. To qualify for the November ballot, petitions signed by the requisite number of registered voters must be submitted to the state in early May – months before any changes to the vote threshold would take effect.

Republicans outnumber Democrats 24-10 in the Senate and 111-51 in the House of Representatives, which has one vacant seat last held by a Democrat. The last day of the legislative session is May 17. The constitutional deadline for granting final passage to the state budget for the 2025 fiscal year, which begins July 1, is one week earlier on May 10.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| **Governor attempts to limit foreign ownership of farmland**
Gov. Mike Parson on Jan. 2 issued an executive order purporting to prohibit people and businesses from countries designated as foreign adversaries from purchasing agricultural land located within 10 miles of a military facility. However, the governor’s action appears to be an unconstitutional attempt to enact legislation via executive order.

Although the executive order doesn’t specify the list of countries deemed to be foreign adversaries, an accompanying news release said those counties include China, Cuba, Iran North Korea, Russia and Venezuela.

The issue of foreign ownership of Missouri farmland has been controversial for more than a decade. Missouri law prohibited any foreign ownership – regardless of whether an adversarial country was involved – until 2013 when the Republican-controlled General Assembly overrode a veto by then-Gov. Jay Nixon, a Democrat, to repeal the ban. Smithfield Foods, which owns several large animal production facilities in Missouri, pushed for the change and shortly thereafter was sold to a Chinese company.

Parson and Lt. Gov. Mike Kehoe, who joined the governor at a press conference announcing the executive order, both voted for the bill and veto override while serving in the state Senate. Kehoe is running in this year’s Republican primary for governor and is backed by Parson, who can’t seek re-election due to term limits.

Democrats opposed repealing the foreign ownership limits and in the years since have sought to restore restrictions. House Minority Leader Crystal Quade, D-Springfield, is one of several lawmakers who have filed legislation this year to do so.

Those bills would limit foreign ownership of agricultural land more tightly than Parson’s limited executive order. Restoring restrictions in state law also would more definitively settle the issue in the event a court strikes down the executive order as an unconstitutional usurpation of legislative power. Parson said he supports legislative action on the matter and that his executive order is intended as a stopgap until that happens.

| **2024 SENATE BILLS**
[**SB 790**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=04a294a203&e=a088917f89) \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm by a minor

[**SB 791**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=cd93712f10&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to homelessness

[**SB 792**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=bdfd5ab432&e=a088917f89) \- Establishes the Revitalizing Missouri Downtowns and Main Streets Act

[**SB 883**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=328803fc65&e=a088917f89) \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions

[**SB 884**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=0bd3e7f85c&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to facilities of historic significance

[**SB 885**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=52af480379&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to school board elections in urban and metropolitan school districts

[**SB 965**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=d0ed5aecd0&e=a088917f89) \- Authorizes a sales tax for special educational services

[**SB 966**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=37a7ee1fb8&e=a088917f89) \- Authorizes a property tax for special educational services

[**SB 967**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=0720b1aedf&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies the calculation of weighted average daily attendance used to calculate state aid for school districts

[**SB 1039**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=c87770c6b3&e=a088917f89) \- Creates the Missouri Geospatial Advisory Council

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=80a1f45ec7&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.

|

If you or someone you know needs help, please visit [Safe Connections](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=c2b3e53acc&e=a088917f89) or call _314-531-2003_.

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|
---
| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. Website](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=0ce335d8ac&e=a088917f89).

clean_body: Legislative session gets underway with low expectations | | | | | | | Capitol Report January 6, 2024 | | | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | Legislative session gets underway with low expectations The 2024 legislative session began Jan. 3 with Republican leaders trying to strike an optimistic tone but hardline conservatives in the Senate making it clear they intend to continue the obstructionism that led to historically low numbers of bills winning final passage in each of the last three years. Although Republicans hold supermajorities in both chambers, GOP infighting has sharply limited their ability to enact legislation. The Senate hardliners first organized in 2021 as the Conservative Caucus but claimed heading into the 2023 session they were disbanding to instead work from within the larger Senate Republican Caucus. In practice, however, nothing changed and the group continued block legislation supported by more mainstream Republicans. This year, they’re operating under the Freedom Caucus banner and began the first day of session by holding the Senate floor for more than an hour to air grievances against GOP leaders. The hardliners started to resume their criticisms on the session’s second day but were cut off when the Senate majority leader moved to adjourn the chamber until next week. For their part, Democratic lawmakers noted that the limited number of bills to win passage last year included several the party had long advocated for and earned bipartisan support. They look to build upon those successes this year to push for targeted tax relief for working families, bolster reproductive rights, improve public school teacher salaries and reduce violent crime by strengthening Missouri’s lax gun laws, among other issues. With an initiative petition being circulated to put a proposed amendment to the state constitution protecting abortion rights on the November ballot, House Republicans said they will make a last-ditch effort to require supermajority approval to ratify amendments. However, any changes proposed by lawmakers also would need to be approved by voters to take effect, and voters in others states have proven resistant to limiting their own power. Even if a higher vote threshold made it on the August ballot and won voter approval, the courts likely would decide whether the changes applied to measures on the November ballot since doing so would amount to changing the rules in the middle of the process. To qualify for the November ballot, petitions signed by the requisite number of registered voters must be submitted to the state in early May – months before any changes to the vote threshold would take effect. Republicans outnumber Democrats 24-10 in the Senate and 111-51 in the House of Representatives, which has one vacant seat last held by a Democrat. The last day of the legislative session is May 17. The constitutional deadline for granting final passage to the state budget for the 2025 fiscal year, which begins July 1, is one week earlier on May 10. | | | | Governor attempts to limit foreign ownership of farmland Gov. Mike Parson on Jan. 2 issued an executive order purporting to prohibit people and businesses from countries designated as foreign adversaries from purchasing agricultural land located within 10 miles of a military facility. However, the governor’s action appears to be an unconstitutional attempt to enact legislation via executive order. Although the executive order doesn’t specify the list of countries deemed to be foreign adversaries, an accompanying news release said those counties include China, Cuba, Iran North Korea, Russia and Venezuela. The issue of foreign ownership of Missouri farmland has been controversial for more than a decade. Missouri law prohibited any foreign ownership – regardless of whether an adversarial country was involved – until 2013 when the Republican-controlled General Assembly overrode a veto by then-Gov. Jay Nixon, a Democrat, to repeal the ban. Smithfield Foods, which owns several large animal production facilities in Missouri, pushed for the change and shortly thereafter was sold to a Chinese company. Parson and Lt. Gov. Mike Kehoe, who joined the governor at a press conference announcing the executive order, both voted for the bill and veto override while serving in the state Senate. Kehoe is running in this year’s Republican primary for governor and is backed by Parson, who can’t seek re-election due to term limits. Democrats opposed repealing the foreign ownership limits and in the years since have sought to restore restrictions. House Minority Leader Crystal Quade, D-Springfield, is one of several lawmakers who have filed legislation this year to do so. Those bills would limit foreign ownership of agricultural land more tightly than Parson’s limited executive order. Restoring restrictions in state law also would more definitively settle the issue in the event a court strikes down the executive order as an unconstitutional usurpation of legislative power. Parson said he supports legislative action on the matter and that his executive order is intended as a stopgap until that happens. | | | | | | | | | | 2024 SENATE BILLS \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm by a minor \- Modifies provisions relating to homelessness \- Establishes the Revitalizing Missouri Downtowns and Main Streets Act \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to facilities of historic significance \- Modifies provisions relating to school board elections in urban and metropolitan school districts \- Authorizes a sales tax for special educational services \- Authorizes a property tax for special educational services \- Modifies the calculation of weighted average daily attendance used to calculate state aid for school districts \- Creates the Missouri Geospatial Advisory Council \- Modifies provisions relating to legal representation for certain court proceedings involving children \- Modifies provisions relating to benevolent tax credits \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to inmate phone call fees \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Repeals provisions relating to the caseload of public defenders \- Modifies provisions relating to delinquent property taxes \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons \- Creates provisions relating to grants for nonprofit organizations at risk for terrorist attacks \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Modifies eligibility for appropriations from the Waterways and Ports Trust Fund | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19). SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing. Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and . body:
| | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76597897/447903987/-312956818?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODI0ZmI4YS1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWwK_1lF5ZwTCmNVYY6Yplrza1TEdqQDw6xDs3alLZM=&emci=34e13a8b-7f9e-ee11-bea1-002248223f36&emdi=3824fb8a-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=6787172) Hey Friend — Three years ago today, violent insurrectionists stormed the U.S. Capitol with the intent of forcefully overturning the results of a free and fair election. A lot’s happened since then — and there’s a lot of ballgame left before this November. **But if we want to save our democracy, we have to take action now.** MAGA extremists are still out there, peddling lies and fighting tooth and nail to undermine voting rights, especially for young Americans. And we don’t know about you, but we’re not going to let them get away with it. **[If you’re with us, will you pledge to vote this November and stop Republicans from undermining our democracy?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76597898/447903988/1146103595?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODI0ZmI4YS1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWwK_1lF5ZwTCmNVYY6Yplrza1TEdqQDw6xDs3alLZM=&emci=34e13a8b-7f9e-ee11-bea1-002248223f36&emdi=3824fb8a-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=6787172)** | [Pledge to vote →](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76597899/447903989/-419980346?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODI0ZmI4YS1hNGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWwK_1lF5ZwTCmNVYY6Yplrza1TEdqQDw6xDs3alLZM=&emci=34e13a8b-7f9e-ee11-bea1-002248223f36&emdi=3824fb8a-a4ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=6787172 "Sign up to text")
---
Doing nothing is not an option.
Being complacent is not an option.
Hoping that the people fighting against us will suddenly change their minds is not an option.
The youth vote is more than capable of protecting our democracy and defending our rights. But we have to show up to the polls to make that possibility a reality.

clean_body: | | Hey Friend — Three years ago today, violent insurrectionists stormed the U.S. Capitol with the intent of forcefully overturning the results of a free and fair election. A lot’s happened since then — and there’s a lot of ballgame left before this November. But if we want to save our democracy, we have to take action now. MAGA extremists are still out there, peddling lies and fighting tooth and nail to undermine voting rights, especially for young Americans. And we don’t know about you, but we’re not going to let them get away with it. | "Sign up to text" Doing nothing is not an option. Being complacent is not an option. Hoping that the people fighting against us will suddenly change their minds is not an option. The youth vote is more than capable of protecting our democracy and defending our rights. But we have to show up to the polls to make that possibility a reality. Donald Trump’s continuous assaults on the foundations of our country drove our democracy to the brink of ruin on January 6, 2021, and he’s threatening to throw our nation into chaos once again.

clean_body: President Joe Biden is calling on us to step up to maintain our democracy! | = | Three years ago, California and the country watched as Donald Trump encouraged a violent mob to storm the United States Capitol and attack law enforcement in a desperate attempt to remain in power. Today, on the three-year anniversary of this insurrection, President Joe Biden is calling on us to step up to maintain our democracy. = = = = = = = We strongly believe that Joe Biden has said it best: | | = In his words: History is watching. The world is watching. Our children and grandchildren will hold us responsible. That’s why we are working our tails off on the ground in California’s most competitive tossup seats earlier than ever. There are enough vulnerable red seats in our state ALONE to flip the U.S. House Majority from red to blue. With a Republican House Majority, Republicans could vote to overturn lawful election results.If we flip the House, we ensure that never happens. Donald Trump’s continuous assaults on the foundations of our country drove our democracy to the brink of ruin on January 6, 2021, and he’s threatening to throw our nation into chaos once again. But we know the value of our democracy and freedoms and California will be on theFRONT LINE of ensuring election results are respected, not overturned. = = Thank you, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. If this email was forwarded to you, We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, And if you no longer wish to receive emails from the California Democratic Party, click here to . | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www.cadem.org | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. California Democratic Party 1830 9th St Sacramento, CA 95811 United States

name: Harry Dunn
subject: Three years ago, I defended the U.S. Capitol from violent insurrectionists. Now, I'm the Democrat running for Congress in MD-03 and I hope you’ll become a Founding Donor of my campaign.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59265?t=6&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU) Hey there, it's Harry Dunn here, one of the Capitol Police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol against MAGA insurrectionists just three years ago and testified in front of the January 6th Committee.

I've got an important announcement: I'm hoping to make my return to DC – but not as a Capitol police officer. I'm officially running to serve Maryland's 3rd Congressional district in the U.S. House.

[**I’m counting on your support to get there. Can you rush $10 or more to become a Founding Donor of my campaign?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59265?t=7&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU) | [BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR: CHIP IN $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59265?t=8&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
---
Here’s the thing, I _never_ thought I would run for office. Growing up, I wanted to be just like my dad, who was an athlete and a U.S. Air Force Veteran. I played football and earned an athletic scholarship that sent me to college. _(I was pretty good, but being 6-foot-7 sure didn’t hurt!)_

But wearing a football uniform taught me something special: it gave me purpose and made me feel a part of something bigger than myself.

So when I put on my Capitol Police uniform for the first time over 15 years ago to protect our nation’s Capitol, _it felt right._

But I never thought I’d see the day that our democracy would be attacked by Donald Trump and an army of insurrectionists that HE directed to the Capitol.

I couldn't have been any closer to the chaos of January 6. I put my life on the line during the violence and destruction that Trump encouraged. So trust and believe when I tell you: **_we cannot allow our democracy to be in the hands of these MAGA-extremists._**

I fought for our democracy and our Capitol once as Officer Dunn, and I'll do it again as Congressman Dunn.

[**Please, can you chip in $10 or $25 to become a Founding Donor of my campaign? I seriously appreciate any help you can give today »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59265?t=9&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Harry Dunn and Voter Protection Project will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59266?t=10&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59267?t=11&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59268?t=12&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59269?t=13&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/59270?t=14&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
Let's do it,

Harry Dunn
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=15&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=12349%2E878179%2EMfhQWU)

name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: We can’t afford to ignore the lessons of the past
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=814%2E52803%2EBadKuX)

| | 3 years ago today, a violent, hateful mob of rioters and insurrectionists stormed the Capitol in an attempt to stop the peaceful transition of power — _one of the very tenets upon which our democracy is built._ We came unspeakably close to losing our democracy that day, Peter. And while we remember that horrific, deadly day in our history, we absolutely must learn from it, because that extremism hasn’t gone away. It’s still present, and anti-democratic rhetoric continues to plague our social media and far-right news outlets. We must remain vigilant to keep something like January 6th from happening again. I’m committed to defending our democracy, Peter, and to expanding opportunity and public safety so that people feel safe in their communities, and to remind extremists that their hateful ideals do NOT represent who we are as Americans. I appreciate everything you do to help represent the love, empathy, and sense of community that truly represents America at its best, Peter. Thank you, Angela Alsobrooks | | | [Donate](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/9340?t=9&amount=25&akid=814%2E52803%2EBadKuX)

name: bumping for Democrats [FFR]
subject: re: January 6th, 2021
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided in kind by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/26910?t=1&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)
---
Hey,

Earlier this week, we sent you our urgent public opinion poll on whether or not Trump should be banned from running for office using the 14th Amendment.

**But our records indicate that you didn’t respond.** Without your response, we’re still [5] local responses short and our dataset for your state is invalid.

Today is the 3rd anniversary of the insurrection and Trump is still fighting tooth and nail to get back into office. **We can’t let that happen.**

Your response is vital to showing undeniable proof that Americans want Trump removed from their state’s ballot for inciting a deadly insurrection. [**Click HERE or use the button below to complete your poll »**](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=7&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e) | **[COMPLETE POLL NOW »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=8&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)**
---
We can’t mark this somber anniversary with more inaction, [**we need you to take the poll now.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=9&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)

I’ve attached the original poll below for your records. Thanks,

Andrew

Fight for Reform
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: MAGA FREAKOUT (via FFR)
Subject: Trump’s name removed from your state ballots?!
To: TOP Democrats
**Trump is PANICKING:**
[ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=10&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)
Now that Maine and Colorado have kicked Trump off the ballot, experts predict that other states could do the same!

**Trump’s re-election scheme would be TOTALLY derailed._YES!!_**

So we’re launching a poll to show that Americans across the country want their state to kick Trump off the ballot and we need you to represent your state:
* * *
_We need 100 local responses before midnight:_
**[Should the 14th Amendment be used to ban Trump from running??](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=11&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)**
[ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=12&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)
**[YES, OF COURSE! »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=13&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)**
---
**[NO, I DON’T THINK SO »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=14&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)**
**[I’M NOT SURE »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/invoke-14A?source=FFR_20240105_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&t=15&akid=20606%2E4449941%2E4Xq-1e)**
_OUR POLL CLOSES PROMPTLY AT MIDNIGHT, WE NEED YOUR RESPONSE NOW._
* * *
Thank you,

– FFR

name: Stop Trump Alert
subject: SIGN ON: Stand with President Biden
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Jan. 6, 2021
| | |
---

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: AIPAC endorses insurrectionists
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Today is the third anniversary of the deadly January 6 attack on the U.S.
Capitol. On this day in 2021, when Congress was scheduled to formally
acknowledge President Biden’s victory in the 2020 presidential election, a
violent mob of right-wing extremists — emboldened by Donald Trump —
stormed the Capitol, sending lawmakers into hiding.

It was a dark, dangerous day for our country as thousands of rioters (some
armed with weapons) mounted an insurrection in an attempt to overturn the
election results, claiming it was “stolen” from Trump. They vandalized the
Capitol and assaulted police officers, with some calling for Vice
President Mike Pence to be hanged.

After the Capitol was cleared and the mess cleaned up, members of Congress
reconvened to authorize the Electoral College votes. Despite everything
that had just happened, 147 Republican legislators chose party over
country and voted against certifying Biden’s election victory.

Since then, AIPAC has endorsed 109 of the 147 Republicans who supported
overturning the 2020 election.

AIPAC, the most powerful anti-Palestinian lobby group bankrolled by
Republican mega-donors and Trump voters, funds and supports
insurrectionists. Why?

Because at the end of the day, AIPAC is a single-issue outside group
willing to overlook our country’s well-being as long as their candidates
unconditionally support Israel and their war crimes — even if they pose a
dangerous threat to our democracy. They even admitted it in 2022 when they
said it is “no moment for the pro-Israel movement to become selective
about its friends."

While AIPAC backs election deniers and extremists, they’re also spending
millions in Democratic primaries trying to defeat progressive candidates,
even incumbents. They’re reportedly planning to spend $100 million to try
and unseat the Squad this year, and they already launched attack ads
against Rashida Tlaib, Jamaal Bowman, and Summer Lee. That’s why we need
your help, Peter.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jdem-20240106.5JU0cJ&refcode=em20240106-32693&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=3&t=1001&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr ]We can’t let AIPAC unseat our bloc of young, working-class, Black and
Brown progressives in Congress fighting for peace and the progress we
deserve. We defeated AIPAC in 2022, and we can do it again, but only if we
come together and act NOW. Make a grassroots contribution of
$3 or more today →

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jdem-20240106.5JU0cJ&refcode=em20240106-32693&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=3&t=1020&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr ]Contribute $3

If AIPAC gets its way, there will be more right-wing extremists in
Congress sowing chaos here and abroad, and less progressives like Rashida,
Jamaal, and Summer standing up for democracy and human rights for all.

Thank you for being with us in this critical fight.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time.

There are other ways to make an impact in support of peace and equality in
Israel and Palestine: please urge your member of Congress to support a
ceasefire through [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/139873?t=1021&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr ]Adalah Justice Project’s petition or by calling them
using [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/139874?t=1022&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr ]Jewish Voice for Peace’s dialer here. Your voice matters in this
critical moment.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20240106-32693-footer&t=1023&refcode2=32693_1203867_w25dUr&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=32693%2E1203867%2Ew25dUr

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

**State Senator Janae Shamp** and her husband were caught on video in the crowd on the Lower Terrace of the Capitol with the crowd that would eventually turn violent and attack the Capitol building. | | Peter,

Happy New Year!

As we dive into 2024, **we must recognize just how much is on the line this year.** Not only do we have a major presidential election — with some primaries as early as January 23! — but we have so many other crucial federal, state, and local races as well.

There are countless issues we could focus on — from gerrymandered congressional maps that strip away Black voting power to anti-LGBTQ+ policies — 2024 could be the most consequential election for Black voters of the _century._

[So we want to hear from YOU, Peter: Going into 2024, what’s _your_ biggest priority?](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnmx1G84qBFAg2F1xj4Dg9I0eSGSuXn82t07kA2t1X22nsRkffow4Skm_vVrkSQuki4J5zh3UyKosDQewva4l-v_q0yQE-zJWZP04cFGd1VuWpzsax5M4gLQjslUUZH60W5vyNoQjv4lOBAsRPOnjLARuiZe2SPzP87qISObtaKiiyeKvkId61-Z5Lj-ldOqRt5dfOAOK0IIjRawc-9SVuSeovhCVpAEjhylU6Uk92cLvfXHHhNHCOQp6Bu7OCLdXp4RSBPN1SKRIdcmgt6z6QcIYtXf181qBGPTYGG-8d9iCvuyHWblj_6hFXBFfzkBQckNcH81aMK_T6FJUPEI1sDBBm272gOpqnOsGU8UVt5q3w/42r/BPUwIcUkSSaCBoKcVoH8kQ/h1/md2zFtCL4o55q-_cAGpS_OzD_HlcGqJCTFfdyCymQIY) | | [2024: CLICK HERE TO SHARE YOUR THOUGHTS](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnmx1G84qBFAg2F1xj4Dg9I0eSGSuXn82t07kA2t1X22nsRkffow4Skm_vVrkSQuki4J5zh3UyKosDQewva4l-v_q0yQE-zJWZP04cFGd1VuWpzsax5M4gLQjslUUZH60W5vyNoQjv4lOBAsRPOnjLARuiZe2SPzP87qISObtaKii_oIDyxfpJUFvYgbwin8Xi09tq18kHfzX9PDi70uMuhccn7NG7CIyrQ21f9gf7MLVmLTUGadiQFHmfGbUwtIe5sci5f08o-HccyziYOAUo_7niN2FzRhCHWTUlWyzRAXIU5qiH5_FkLml81Y6UEmOYUqr4IOSCZkF5nwtmmdfR6QHyPWCkaEHOiTy0aY_eSa-g/42r/BPUwIcUkSSaCBoKcVoH8kQ/h2/kWXySwf98L69ukbd5M5Hcm-4-OgNtnsz38vkvptYb3I)

Thank you for your input,

The Collective PAC

* * *

[](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w5O3Wwmdygae0W-34efSvb_hfX51xtIkq_nCgr0tl8iRqUTOndcJRC4vm20WcsKLi4Yf2OonH1Hw9lj8rfq35h2ak82WSopB5jJtW9hf-PKgwNhotBXJTI7Gf-TWvxIk2U3faB7ldc88g4Xcw55P1qs2HYvAzqGN6cq8FFhA8HpUvNtlXrmCGz8hLXRJBUyMFEC_S_49mteakbI7mBZQG1FJGmK56eDU_ddW5YSmJmmOACvxIrATBLh-NBkNt4Edp4QDGVkdAJO2oxXIya-chDnp63wBgkQcMRWBKoIdRGI7km_h66GtJ4BODfjAzEus4Q7NaRg0PdQUd-r1ire-8Hoe1B9zmj0MGNTfE_QbHyDmQ5E1BccMSlQc_5f8ic51OgKRhtfqrtxBirdPcG-SJg/42r/BPUwIcUkSSaCBoKcVoH8kQ/h3/e8D69O4EYNDwIVtKYBlXXlMA7avdAGczlic9cveBe-E) The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people.

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. There are countless issues we could focus on — from gerrymandered congressional maps that strip away Black voting power to anti-LGBTQ+ policies — 2024 could be the most consequential election for Black voters of the century. | | Thank you for your input, The Collective PAC The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people. name: Powered By People
subject: Take a look!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
In Texas, one must be trained and certified as a Volunteer Deputy Registrar (VDR) in order to register their neighbors to vote. So to help Democrats win elections in our state, Powered by People is holding multiple VDR trainings _every single week._
When we host each training, dozens and sometimes hundreds of people sign up to get VDR certified and hit the ground running registering voters. **Take a look at some of our volunteers with their fresh VDR certificates!**
**[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240106.Ib2lTz&refcode=ema20240106-237401&t=1&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM)**
[**Can you chip in $3 today to help us train more VDRs to register Texas voters?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240106.Ib2lTz&refcode=ema20240106-237401&t=2&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM)
**[DONATE $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240106.Ib2lTz&refcode=ema20240106-237401&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM)**
After our volunteers earn their VDR certificates, they’ll hold voter registration events on their college campuses, at their farmer’s markets, and anywhere else they can find eligible but unregistered Democrats. They’ll even canvass their neighborhoods to register voters directly at their doorsteps.
This is the tough work that’s necessary to move the needle in Texas and change the future of our country. [**So thank you for chipping in today to help us keep up this momentum.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240106.Ib2lTz&refcode=ema20240106-237401&t=22&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM)
Powered by People

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20240106-237401-footer&t=23&refcode2=237401_444211_C6dCAM&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM)**
Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople.org](http://www.poweredxpeople.org?akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM), not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We are powered by grassroots supporters like you. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=237401%2E444211%2EC6dCAM).

# [This Jan 6th hero cop is running for Congress. Thank him for defending democracy](https://default.salsalabs.org/T05b2d062-6dab-4d5f-9338-a0fca9e9b077/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) and [make a donation if you can >](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0d4ff316-8ed6-43c6-a599-f9e73591f379/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | As a Capitol Police officer, Harry Dunn defended the Capitol and those working there when it was attacked on January 6th. Now [he's running for Congress](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3376caf6-33da-4da5-ba6f-a061d2b8b932/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), where he will once again defend our democracy. **Please[join us in thanking him for defending democracy](https://default.salsalabs.org/T806afe7f-8417-4f3d-bb88-c5a124306497/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**
* **If you can, make a[donation to help him win](https://default.salsalabs.org/Teab6a3a7-ad3d-4c9d-a533-0fb3196bf246/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)! **
* **And consider making it a[monthly recurring donation](https://default.salsalabs.org/T29dbdc46-7c63-4aee-9337-8cb289478d4e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)!**

name: Emilia Sykes
subject: Three years ago today.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emilia Sykes for Congress
body:
The fight for democracy lives on.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601192/447914995/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMGM2NWM3Zi1hOGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=REzBUDrPTWeOljs2tgfwxjATTPqFwTJij6uRsWCN7B8=&emci=b52e8a96-a5ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=00c65c7f-a8ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=11703046) Dear Peter,

On January 6, 2021, a violent mob of insurrectionists attacked the U.S. Capitol. I continue to thank the brave U.S. Capitol Police and other first responders who safeguarded our democracy and protected our nation's capital on that dark day. January 6 was more than an attack on our capital — it was an attack on the very foundation of our democracy.

And now, three years later the work to protect our democracy lives on. But we must work together to protect it. So much is on the line this November — it is clear that it is up to us to defend the fabric of our nation.

_That’s why it is so critical that we come together this year to push back._ Holding Ohio’s 13th Congressional District is critical to our democracy, giving us the opportunity to guarantee and expand voting rights, ensure we have free and fair elections, and defeat the extremist agenda to dismantle our democratic institutions.

Our democracy is worth defending — it's our shared responsibility as American citizens. I know we will do it together.

Yours In Service,

_Emilia_
---
| Emilia Sykes is running for re-election in Ohio's 13th Congressional District. To learn more, please visit [EmiliaSykesForCongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601193/447914996/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMGM2NWM3Zi1hOGFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=REzBUDrPTWeOljs2tgfwxjATTPqFwTJij6uRsWCN7B8=&emci=b52e8a96-a5ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=00c65c7f-a8ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=11703046)

name: Indiana Democratic Party
subject: Never Again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Peter, Three short years ago today, former President Donald Trump, his allies in Congress, and a mob of his supporters attempted to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election. Hoosiers, along with tens of millions of Americans watched the scenes on their screens with Trump supporters violently breaking into the U.S. Capitol and attacking law enforcement officers. Our democracy stood the test on that day, but we must never allow what happened on January 6, 2021 to happen again. **[Here in Indiana, Jim Banks, one of January 6th’s cheerleaders, wants a promotion to the Senate. Together, we need to stop him and his MAGA agenda.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601802/447917241/405560545?refcode=240106NeverAgain&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWMzOTYyMS1hOWFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csIAVKfUf_yp3oHi8E9QHM7CHa_ZYHCh7Y2I1xI7E_A=)** Democracy is one again on the ballot in 2024. Hoosiers, along with tens of millions of Americans watched the scenes on their screens with Trump supporters violently breaking into the U.S. Capitol and attacking law enforcement officers. Our democracy stood the test on that day, but we must never allow what happened on January 6, 2021 to happen again. = Democracy is one again on the ballot in 2024. We can’t allow the levers of power to a party that wants to throw out the votes of millions of Americans. = Thanks, Indiana Democratic Party | | | = | = [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. On January 6, 2021, Marsha Blackburn wanted to help overturn the presidential election.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601843/447917371/-849459103?refcode=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ms=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWMzOTYyMS1hOWFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EvwpaPPOdHnWvs2SDCtmcSDnb-sAPUdXH267P8X9h3o=&emci=9e9c3a27-08ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=46764483)

Three years ago, the peaceful transfer of power — a cornerstone of our democracy — was threatened when right-wing extremists descended on Washington, D.C., and overran the U.S. Capitol. All because politicians stoked the flames of insurrection with lies and misinformation. Marsha Blackburn helped Donald Trump incite that far-right violence. And she knew better. MAGA Marsha aided Donald Trump in his attempt to overthrow the 2020 election and reject the will of the people. Before insurrectionists stormed the Capitol, Marsha announced that she intended to _object_ to certifying the electoral count. There was no evidence behind the Big Lie of election fraud, but Marsha was ready to support it anyway... _because she knew that’s what Donald Trump’s MAGA base expected._ So far, Marsha hasn’t been held accountable for her role in perpetuating the Big Lie and the extremism that led to the violence on January 6th, 2021. It’s up to us to do so in 2024. **_[We need to gauge how Tennessee voters view Marsha Blackburn. Peter, do you believe she is qualified to serve as senator for Tennessee?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601844/447917372/-1760169375?refcode=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ms=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWMzOTYyMS1hOWFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EvwpaPPOdHnWvs2SDCtmcSDnb-sAPUdXH267P8X9h3o=&emci=9e9c3a27-08ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=46764483)_** | [**YES**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/76601845/447917373/-1760169375?refcode=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ms=ea.6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWMzOTYyMS1hOWFjLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EvwpaPPOdHnWvs2SDCtmcSDnb-sAPUdXH267P8X9h3o=&emci=9e9c3a27-08ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=6ec39621-a9ac-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=46764483)

Peter – My name is Harry Dunn and three years ago today, I defended the U.S. Capitol and our nation’s leaders against a violent insurrection.

_Since that day, I vowed to help ensure that never happens again._

**That’s why I’m officially running for Congress in MD-03 to be the change I want to see for our country and our democracy.**[I’d be honored for you to join me and Defeat Republicans in jump starting this campaign by chipping in a split donation of $25 or anything you can: ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76601916/447917623/-1538229440?refcode=DR_SPLIT_FR_90DO_240106_1_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNi8xLzg1ODM1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0NzdlMjI3LWE5YWMtZWUxMS1iZWExLTAwMjI0ODIyMzdkYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Y7P0HeE-nJKD7VI-1zmpe4NQiGEUxj7aI_4wya07yig=)
| **[BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76601925/447917633/-1538229440?refcode=DR_SPLIT_FR_90DO_240106_1_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNi8xLzg1ODM1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0NzdlMjI3LWE5YWMtZWUxMS1iZWExLTAwMjI0ODIyMzdkYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Y7P0HeE-nJKD7VI-1zmpe4NQiGEUxj7aI_4wya07yig=)**
---

For the last sixteen years, I was a police officer for the United States Capitol where I saw millions of people visit to exercise their rights and lobby their representatives.

_And trust me, every political agenda you can think of walked through those doors._

It didn’t matter to me if you were there as a Democrat, Republican, or Independent though, because my job was the same - protect our elected officials and ensure everyone could safely participate in our democracy.

But three years ago today, under the Capitol Rotunda that took my breath away each day, I was immersed in a profane mix of sweat, screams, shrieks, anger, death, fear, blood, broken limbs, spit, hatred, horror, racism, bigotry, and heroism for what felt like endless hours alongside fellow officers.

It was unlike anything we had seen before. Sure, there were heated political moments over the years. But this was different.

It was pepper spray, bear spray, and even WD-40 to our faces as we fought back rioters in tactical gear using anything they could as a weapon. It was disorienting chaos as we tried to block every entrance while our elected leaders and capitol staff ran to safety. **It was a direct attack on our democracy.**

Many people have tried to deny what happened that day – including some of the same people who stood behind us as we protected them. They’ve diluted the story and muddied the facts to preserve their own political agenda.

So I’ve made it a personal mission to share my story, speak truth to power, and work to ensure this _never_ happens again. That’s why a few weeks ago, I retired from the police force, and why yesterday – I announced my campaign to run for Congress in Maryland’s Third Congressional District.

**I’m running because Maryland deserves a Congressman who will put the country above their party, and put the constitution above themselves.** [This will be a competitive race, so I’ve set an ambitious goal to raise $500,000 within the first 24 hours of my campaign. Will you chip in to become a Founding Donor and help hit our goal before midnight? ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/76601931/447917643/-1538229440?refcode=DR_SPLIT_FR_90DO_240106_1_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNi8xLzg1ODM1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0NzdlMjI3LWE5YWMtZWUxMS1iZWExLTAwMjI0ODIyMzdkYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Y7P0HeE-nJKD7VI-1zmpe4NQiGEUxj7aI_4wya07yig=)

clean_body: | = Peter – My name is Harry Dunn and three years ago today, I defended the U.S. Capitol and our nation’s leaders against a violent insurrection. Since that day, I vowed to help ensure that never happens again. That’s why I’m officially running for Congress in MD-03 to be the change I want to see for our country and our democracy.= | = For the last sixteen years, I was a police officer for the United States Capitol where I saw millions of people visit to exercise their rights and lobby their representatives. And trust me, every political agenda you can think of walked through those doors. It didn’t matter to me if you were there as a Democrat, Republican, or Independent though, because my job was the same - protect our elected officials and ensure everyone could safely participate in our democracy. But three years ago today, under the Capitol Rotunda that took my breath away each day, I was immersed in a profane mix of sweat, screams, shrieks, anger, death, fear, blood, broken limbs, spit, hatred, horror, racism, bigotry, and heroism for what felt like endless hours alongside fellow officers. It was unlike anything we had seen before. Sure, there were heated political moments over the years. But this was different. It was pepper spray, bear spray, and even WD-40 to our faces as we fought back rioters in tactical gear using anything they could as a weapon. It was disorienting chaos as we tried to block every entrance while our elected leaders and capitol staff ran to safety. It was a direct attack on our democracy. Many people have tried to deny what happened that day – including some of the same people who stood behind us as we protected them. They’ve diluted the story and muddied the facts to preserve their own political agenda. So I’ve made it a personal mission to share my story, speak truth to power, and work to ensure this never happens again. That’s why a few weeks ago, I retired from the police force, and why yesterday – I announced my campaign to run for Congress in Maryland’s Third Congressional District. I’m running because Maryland deserves a Congressman who will put the country above their party, and put the constitution above themselves. = = Thank you for reading, Harry Dunn ****** **** **** **** **** ****** Defeat Republicans | Paid for by Defeat Republicans PAC (www.DefeatRepublicans.org) and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
